examples: The dots on Madame Boudin 9; s dress are actually grains of sand that blew onto the canvas as Monet painted.
Painted on Monet's honeymoon, this canvas depicts his wife and Madame Boudin at Trouville, on the Normandy coast.
This picture is one of number of similar views of the harbour at Brussels painted by Boudin in the winter of 1870-1, after he had moved there to escape the Franco-Prussian war.
Boudin was called the "king of skies" and it is easy to see why.
Born in Honfleur, Boudin didn't stray far from the English Channel seacoast until late-life travels to Venice and Istanbul.
